svg.title: DNA Replication Fork — Molecular Biology Textbook Illustration

g.text: Unwinds parental dsDNA by breaking H-bonds between base pairs
Relieves positive supercoiling ahead of the replication fork
Stabilize single-stranded DNA; prevent re-annealing and degradation
Synthesizes short RNA primers to initiate DNA synthesis
Main replicative polymerase; extends primers with new DNA (5' to 3')
Ring-shaped processivity factor; tethers Pol III to the DNA template
Removes RNA primers (5'→3' exonuclease) and fills gaps with DNA
Seals nicks between Okazaki fragments by forming phosphodiester bonds

svg.text: Figure: DNA Replication Fork | Prokaryotic Model (E. coli) | Direction of replication: 5' → 3'
